def: The hard, mineralised outer layer covering the crown of a tooth.
ex: Acidic drinks gradually erode the protective {{enamel}} on the teeth.

def: The dense, calcified tissue forming the bulk of a tooth beneath the enamel.
ex: When enamel wears away, the sensitive {{dentine}} beneath is exposed.

def: The soft inner tissue of a tooth containing nerves and blood vessels.
ex: Deep decay can reach the {{pulp}} and cause severe pain.

def: The thin bone-like tissue covering the root surface of a tooth.
ex: The {{cementum}} anchors the periodontal fibres to the tooth root.

def: The visible part of a tooth above the gum line.
ex: The {{crown}} of the tooth is covered by a layer of enamel.

def: The part of a tooth embedded in the jawbone below the gum.
ex: Each molar {{root}} is held firmly within the alveolar bone.

def: A flat front tooth with a sharp edge used for cutting food.
ex: The upper central {{incisor}} is the widest of the front teeth.

def: A pointed tooth beside the incisors used for tearing food.
ex: The {{canine}} has a single cusp and a long, sturdy root.

def: A transitional tooth between the canines and molars used for grinding.
ex: Each {{premolar}} usually bears two cusps for crushing food.

def: A broad back tooth with a flat surface for grinding food.
ex: The first {{molar}} erupts around the age of six.

def: The third and last molar that erupts in early adulthood.
ex: An impacted {{wisdom tooth}} often needs surgical extraction.

def: The firm pink tissue surrounding the base of the teeth.
ex: Healthy {{gum}} tissue fits snugly around each tooth.

def: The medical term for the gum tissue surrounding the teeth.
ex: Inflammation of the {{gingiva}} is the first sign of gum disease.

def: Fibrous tissue connecting the tooth root to the alveolar bone.
ex: The {{periodontal ligament}} cushions the tooth against biting forces.

def: The part of the jaw that holds the tooth sockets.
ex: Bone loss in the {{alveolar bone}} loosens the affected teeth.

def: Either of the two bony structures holding the teeth.
ex: The lower {{jaw}} is the only movable bone of the skull.

def: The lower jawbone, which holds the lower teeth.
ex: The {{mandible}} forms the movable lower part of the face.

def: The upper jawbone, which holds the upper teeth.
ex: The {{maxilla}} forms the fixed upper part of the dental arch.

def: The roof of the mouth separating the oral and nasal cavities.
ex: The hard {{palate}} forms the bony front of the mouth's roof.

def: The watery fluid in the mouth that aids chewing and digestion.
ex: {{Saliva}} helps neutralise acids and protect the teeth.

def: A gland that produces and secretes saliva into the mouth.
ex: A blocked {{salivary gland}} can cause painful swelling.

def: The curved arrangement of teeth in either jaw.
ex: Crowding occurs when teeth outgrow the {{dental arch}}.

def: The manner in which the upper and lower teeth meet.
ex: A balanced {{occlusion}} distributes chewing forces evenly.

def: A misalignment of the teeth when the jaws are closed.
ex: Orthodontic braces are used to correct a {{malocclusion}}.

def: The way the upper and lower teeth come together.
ex: The dentist checked the child's {{bite}} for alignment problems.

def: A raised point on the chewing surface of a tooth.
ex: The molar's fractured {{cusp}} needed to be restored.

def: The natural cavity within a tooth root containing the pulp.
ex: Infected pulp was removed during the {{root canal}} treatment.

def: The bony cavity in the jaw that holds a tooth root.
ex: A blood clot forms in the {{tooth socket}} after extraction.

def: The first set of temporary teeth in young children.
ex: A child's {{milk teeth}} begin to fall out around age six.

def: The adult set of teeth that replaces the milk teeth.
ex: Adults normally have thirty-two {{permanent teeth}} in total.

def: The clinical term for the first, temporary set of teeth.
ex: There are twenty {{deciduous teeth}} in a full primary set.

def: The arrangement, type and number of teeth in the mouth.
ex: A child's mixed {{dentition}} contains both baby and adult teeth.

def: A hard structure in the jaws used for biting and chewing.
ex: A decayed {{tooth}} may require a filling or crown.

def: The narrow region where the crown meets the tooth root.
ex: Gum recession exposes the {{neck of the tooth}} to sensitivity.

def: The tip of a tooth root where nerves and vessels enter.
ex: An abscess had formed around the {{apex}} of the root.

def: The internal space of a tooth housing the dental pulp.
ex: The nerve runs through the {{pulp cavity}} into the root.

def: The hollow interior space of the mouth.
ex: The dentist examined the entire {{oral cavity}} for lesions.

def: The muscular organ in the mouth used for tasting and speech.
ex: The {{tongue}} helps move food toward the teeth during chewing.

def: Either of the two soft folds forming the mouth's opening.
ex: The upper {{lip}} covers the front surface of the incisors.

def: The soft fleshy side of the face forming the mouth's wall.
ex: The inner {{cheek}} lining is called the buccal mucosa.

def: The moist inner lining of the cheeks inside the mouth.
ex: A white patch appeared on the patient's {{buccal mucosa}}.

def: A small fold of tissue tethering the lip or tongue.
ex: A tight lingual {{frenulum}} can restrict tongue movement.

def: The hinge joint connecting the lower jaw to the skull.
ex: Clicking in the {{temporomandibular joint}} may signal a disorder.

def: The border where the gum tissue meets the teeth.
ex: Plaque accumulates along the {{gum line}} if left uncleaned.

def: The basic structural unit of tooth enamel.
ex: Each {{enamel rod}} runs from the dentine toward the surface.

def: A microscopic channel running through the dentine of a tooth.
ex: Cold stimuli travel along each {{dentinal tubule}} to the nerve.

def: The biting surface of a premolar or molar tooth.
ex: Decay often begins in the grooves of the {{occlusal surface}}.

def: A natural groove on the chewing surface of a tooth.
ex: A deep {{fissure}} traps food and encourages plaque buildup.

def: A sticky film of bacteria that forms on the teeth.
ex: Regular brushing removes {{dental plaque}} before it hardens.

def: Hardened dental plaque that has mineralised on the teeth.
ex: The hygienist scraped away the {{tartar}} near the gum line.

def: The tissue in the pulp that transmits pain and sensation.
ex: The dental {{nerve}} was exposed by the deep cavity.

def: A tube supplying blood to the pulp and surrounding tissues.
ex: Tiny {{blood vessel}} networks nourish the living dental pulp.

def: A small nipple-like projection of tissue, as on the tongue.
ex: The gum {{papilla}} fills the space between adjacent teeth.

def: The fleshy tissue hanging at the back of the soft palate.
ex: The {{uvula}} dangles at the rear of the oral cavity.

def: The muscular rear portion of the roof of the mouth.
ex: The {{soft palate}} rises to seal off the nasal passage.

def: The bony front part of the roof of the mouth.
ex: The tongue presses against the {{hard palate}} when swallowing.

def: The shallow groove between the tooth and the surrounding gum.
ex: Bacteria collecting in the {{gingival sulcus}} can inflame the gum.

def: The layer of enamel covering the crown of a tooth.
ex: The thin {{enamel cap}} protects the underlying dentine from wear.

def: The line where the enamel and cementum meet on a tooth.
ex: Gum recession is measured from the {{cementoenamel junction}}.

def: Another name for the pointed canine tooth.
ex: The {{cuspid}} guides the jaw during side-to-side movement.

def: A premolar tooth bearing two cusps for grinding food.
ex: The {{bicuspid}} sits between the canine and the molars.

def: The sac of tissue surrounding a developing tooth before eruption.
ex: The {{dental follicle}} gives rise to the tooth's supporting tissues.

def: The early cellular structure from which a tooth develops.
ex: Each {{tooth bud}} forms long before the tooth erupts.

def: The process by which a tooth emerges through the gum.
ex: The {{eruption}} of the first molar occurs around age six.

def: The upper surface of a tooth used to grind food.
ex: Food debris often lodges in the {{chewing surface}} grooves.

def: The outermost visible layer on the crown of a tooth.
ex: Fluoride strengthens the {{tooth enamel surface}} against decay.

def: The very tip of a tooth root inside the jawbone.
ex: An infection near the {{root apex}} may form an abscess.

def: The area where the roots of a multi-rooted tooth divide.
ex: Bone loss reaching the {{furcation}} weakens a molar significantly.

def: The surface of a tooth facing towards the tongue.
ex: Tartar tends to build up on the {{lingual surface}} of incisors.

def: The surface of a back tooth facing the cheek.
ex: Plaque was visible on the {{buccal surface}} of the molars.

def: The overall shape of the curved row of teeth.
ex: The orthodontist assessed the patient's {{dental arch form}}.

def: The moist tissue lining the inside of the mouth.
ex: The oral {{mucous membrane}} protects the underlying tissues.

def: The space between the lips or cheeks and the teeth.
ex: The dentist retracted the lip to inspect the {{vestibule}}.

def: A hole in a tooth caused by decay that destroys enamel and dentine.
ex: The dentist found a small {{cavity}} on the chewing surface of the molar.

def: A bacterial disease that progressively demineralises and destroys the hard tissues of teeth.
ex: Poor oral hygiene is the leading cause of {{dental caries}} in children.

def: The gradual destruction of tooth structure caused by acids from bacterial plaque.
ex: Frequent sugar consumption accelerates {{tooth decay}} on exposed surfaces.

def: Calcified deposit of mineralised bacterial plaque adhering to the tooth surface.
ex: Subgingival {{dental calculus}} was removed during the scaling appointment.

def: Reversible inflammation of the gums causing redness, swelling and bleeding on brushing.
ex: Her bleeding gums were an early sign of {{gingivitis}}.

def: Advanced gum disease destroying the bone and tissues that support the teeth.
ex: Untreated gingivitis can progress to {{periodontitis}} and eventual tooth loss.

def: A group of inflammatory conditions affecting the supporting tissues around the teeth.
ex: Smoking significantly increases the risk of {{periodontal disease}}.

def: A localised collection of pus caused by a bacterial infection in or around a tooth.
ex: The patient presented with a painful swelling from a {{dental abscess}}.

def: Persistent unpleasant breath odour, often linked to bacteria and poor oral hygiene.
ex: Chronic {{halitosis}} may indicate underlying periodontal infection.

def: Excessive vertical overlap of the upper front teeth over the lower teeth.
ex: A deep {{overbite}} can cause the lower teeth to touch the palate.

def: A condition where the lower teeth protrude beyond the upper front teeth.
ex: The child's {{underbite}} was corrected with early orthodontic intervention.

def: A misalignment where upper teeth bite inside the lower teeth when closing.
ex: A posterior {{crossbite}} may lead to uneven jaw growth if untreated.

def: A malocclusion in which upper and lower teeth do not meet when closed.
ex: Prolonged thumb-sucking contributed to the patient's anterior {{open bite}}.

def: The horizontal distance by which the upper front teeth project beyond the lower.
ex: An increased {{overjet}} makes the front teeth more prone to trauma.

def: Short sharp pain in teeth triggered by hot, cold, or sweet stimuli.
ex: She complained of {{tooth sensitivity}} whenever she drank cold water.

def: Sharp transient pain arising from exposed dentine in response to external stimuli.
ex: Exposed root surfaces are a common cause of {{dentine hypersensitivity}}.

def: Loss of tooth enamel caused by acids not involving bacterial action.
ex: Frequent consumption of citrus juice caused noticeable {{dental erosion}}.

def: Wearing away of tooth surfaces caused by tooth-to-tooth contact during function.
ex: Years of grinding produced marked {{attrition}} on the incisal edges.

def: Loss of tooth structure from mechanical wear other than chewing, such as brushing.
ex: Aggressive brushing caused {{abrasion}} notches near the gumline.

def: Wedge-shaped loss of tooth near the gumline caused by occlusal stress.
ex: The clinician attributed the cervical lesions to {{abfraction}} from heavy biting forces.

def: A tooth that fails to erupt fully because it is blocked or misaligned.
ex: The {{impacted tooth}} had to be surgically removed under local anaesthetic.

def: A tooth with a fracture line that may cause pain on biting.
ex: A {{cracked tooth}} often causes sharp pain when releasing a bite.

def: Gradual pulling back of gum tissue that exposes the tooth root.
ex: {{Receding gums}} left the sensitive root surfaces exposed to the air.

def: Apical migration of the gum margin exposing the cementum of the root.
ex: Localised {{gingival recession}} was noted on the lower canines.

def: Abnormal loosening of a tooth due to loss of supporting bone or ligament.
ex: Increased {{tooth mobility}} is a warning sign of advanced periodontitis.

def: A malignant tumour arising in the tissues of the mouth or oropharynx.
ex: Early detection greatly improves the survival rate for {{oral cancer}}.

def: A condition of reduced saliva that leaves the mouth persistently dry.
ex: Many medications cause {{dry mouth}}, raising the risk of decay.

def: The subjective sensation of a dry mouth resulting from reduced salivary flow.
ex: Radiotherapy to the head and neck frequently induces {{xerostomia}}.

def: Involuntary grinding or clenching of the teeth, often during sleep.
ex: Nighttime {{bruxism}} had worn down the surfaces of her molars.

def: Abnormal change in the colour of a tooth from various intrinsic or extrinsic causes.
ex: Trauma to the front tooth caused gradual {{tooth discolouration}}.

def: An X-ray image used to diagnose conditions of the teeth and jawbone.
ex: The {{dental radiograph}} revealed decay hidden between the two molars.

def: Inflammation of the dental pulp, commonly causing severe toothache.
ex: Irreversible {{pulpitis}} usually requires root canal treatment.

def: A pus-filled infection at the tip of a tooth root following pulp death.
ex: The {{periapical abscess}} appeared as a dark shadow on the radiograph.

def: Decay affecting the exposed root surface, common in older adults.
ex: {{Root caries}} developed on the exposed surfaces after gum recession.

def: A developmental defect resulting in thin or deficient tooth enamel.
ex: Childhood illness can lead to {{enamel hypoplasia}} in permanent teeth.

def: Enamel discolouration and mottling caused by excessive fluoride during tooth development.
ex: Mild {{dental fluorosis}} appears as faint white flecks on the enamel.

def: A white patch on the oral mucosa that cannot be rubbed off.
ex: The persistent {{leukoplakia}} was biopsied to exclude malignancy.

def: A fungal infection of the mouth caused by overgrowth of Candida.
ex: Denture wearers are prone to {{oral candidiasis}} under the appliance.

def: A small painful shallow ulcer on the soft tissues inside the mouth.
ex: A recurrent {{aphthous ulcer}} formed on the inside of her cheek.

def: A blister on or around the lips caused by the herpes simplex virus.
ex: A {{cold sore}} erupted on his lip after a fever.

def: The condition of having lost all natural teeth in an arch.
ex: Complete {{edentulism}} was managed with a full set of dentures.

def: A visible gap or space between two adjacent teeth, usually the incisors.
ex: The midline {{diastema}} between the front teeth was closed orthodontically.

def: Insufficient space in the jaw causing teeth to overlap or twist.
ex: Severe {{dental crowding}} made it hard to clean between her teeth.

def: An extra tooth that develops beyond the normal number of teeth.
ex: A {{supernumerary tooth}} was blocking the eruption of the incisor.

def: The developmental absence of one or a few teeth.
ex: {{Hypodontia}} of the lateral incisors runs in the patient's family.

def: The rare congenital absence of all teeth, primary or permanent.
ex: Complete {{anodontia}} is usually associated with an ectodermal disorder.

def: Inflammation of the gum flap overlying a partially erupted tooth.
ex: {{Pericoronitis}} around the wisdom tooth caused pain and swelling.

def: A drainage channel from a dental infection to the mucosal surface.
ex: A {{sinus tract}} on the gum was draining pus from the infected root.

def: A disorder affecting the jaw joint and the muscles controlling movement.
ex: Her jaw pain and clicking were diagnosed as a {{temporomandibular disorder}}.

def: Restricted opening of the mouth caused by spasm of the jaw muscles.
ex: Postoperative {{trismus}} limited the patient's mouth opening for days.

def: Inflammation and cracking at the corners of the mouth.
ex: {{Angular cheilitis}} developed at the corners of the elderly patient's mouth.

def: Inflammation of the tongue causing swelling, redness and smoothness.
ex: Vitamin deficiency can produce a painful {{glossitis}} of the tongue.

def: A benign condition with irregular smooth red patches on the tongue.
ex: The map-like patches were typical of a harmless {{geographic tongue}}.

def: Painful inflammation and ulceration of the mucous membranes inside the mouth.
ex: Chemotherapy caused severe {{oral mucositis}} that made eating difficult.

def: General inflammation of the mucous lining of any structure in the mouth.
ex: Denture-related {{stomatitis}} presented as redness under the upper plate.

def: An abnormally deepened gap between the tooth and gum caused by disease.
ex: Probing revealed a five-millimetre {{periodontal pocket}} around the molar.

def: Gums that bleed easily, usually an early sign of gum inflammation.
ex: {{Bleeding gums}} while brushing are often the first sign of gingivitis.

def: A break in the hard structure of a tooth caused by trauma or stress.
ex: A fall caused a vertical {{tooth fracture}} of the upper incisor.

def: Complete displacement of a tooth out of its socket following trauma.
ex: The {{dental avulsion}} of the front tooth required urgent re-implantation.

def: Displacement of a tooth within its socket without complete removal.
ex: Lateral {{luxation}} of the incisor followed the sports injury.

def: Death of the soft tissue inside a tooth following irreversible damage.
ex: The {{necrotic pulp}} showed no response to the vitality test.

def: A tooth whose pulp has died and no longer has a blood supply.
ex: The darkened {{non-vital tooth}} required root canal treatment.

def: New decay that develops at the margins of an existing filling or restoration.
ex: {{Secondary caries}} was found leaking beneath the old amalgam filling.

def: Decay occurring in the pits and fissures of the biting surfaces.
ex: {{Occlusal caries}} commonly begins in the deep grooves of molars.

def: A red velvety patch of oral mucosa with high malignant potential.
ex: The {{erythroplakia}} on the floor of the mouth warranted an urgent biopsy.

def: Progressive loss of tooth substance from erosion, attrition or abrasion combined.
ex: Advanced {{tooth wear}} had flattened the cusps of his back teeth.

def: Material placed into a cavity to restore a decayed tooth's shape and function.
ex: The dentist removed the decay and placed a composite {{dental filling}}.

def: The removal of a tooth from its socket in the alveolar bone.
ex: A severe infection made {{tooth extraction}} the only viable option.

def: Endodontic procedure removing infected pulp and sealing the tooth's inner canals.
ex: The abscessed molar required {{root canal treatment}} to save it.

def: A cap that fully covers a damaged tooth to restore strength and appearance.
ex: After the root canal, they fitted a porcelain {{dental crown}}.

def: A fixed prosthesis replacing missing teeth by anchoring to adjacent teeth.
ex: A three-unit {{dental bridge}} filled the gap left by the lost tooth.

def: A titanium post surgically fixed into the jawbone to support a replacement tooth.
ex: The surgeon placed a {{dental implant}} to anchor the new crown.

def: A removable appliance replacing missing teeth and surrounding oral tissues.
ex: The patient was fitted with a full upper {{denture}}.

def: A thin shell bonded to a tooth's front surface to improve appearance.
ex: A porcelain {{veneer}} corrected the discoloured front tooth.

def: Removal of plaque and calculus from tooth surfaces above and below the gumline.
ex: The hygienist performed {{scaling}} to remove hardened tartar.

def: Smoothing tooth surfaces after scaling to remove stains and hinder plaque.
ex: After scaling, gentle {{polishing}} left the enamel smooth and clean.

def: Topical application of fluoride to strengthen enamel and prevent decay.
ex: Children received a {{fluoride treatment}} to protect against cavities.

def: A protective coating applied to molar grooves to prevent decay.
ex: A {{dental sealant}} was painted onto the child's molars.

def: A drug injected to numb a specific area during dental procedures.
ex: The dentist administered a {{local anaesthetic}} before drilling.

def: A high-speed handpiece used to remove decay and shape teeth.
ex: The whine of the {{dental drill}} made the patient tense.

def: The dental specialty correcting misaligned teeth and jaw irregularities.
ex: She trained in {{orthodontics}} to straighten patients' teeth.

def: Fixed appliances of brackets and wires that gradually straighten teeth.
ex: The teenager got metal {{braces}} to close the gaps.

def: An appliance worn after braces to hold teeth in their corrected positions.
ex: He must wear a {{retainer}} nightly to prevent relapse.

def: A removable transparent tray that gradually moves teeth into alignment.
ex: She chose a {{clear aligner}} instead of visible metal braces.

def: The dental specialty treating diseases of the dental pulp and root canals.
ex: Complex root canals are referred to {{endodontics}} specialists.

def: The dental specialty concerned with gums and supporting tooth structures.
ex: Advanced gum disease is managed within {{periodontics}}.

def: The specialty restoring and replacing teeth with crowns, bridges and dentures.
ex: Full-mouth rehabilitation falls under {{prosthodontics}}.

def: Surgical procedures on the mouth, jaws and associated facial structures.
ex: Impacted wisdom teeth were removed under {{oral surgery}}.

def: Practices keeping the mouth clean to prevent decay and gum disease.
ex: Good {{dental hygiene}} includes brushing and flossing daily.

def: A cosmetic procedure lightening tooth colour using bleaching agents.
ex: Professional {{tooth whitening}} removed years of coffee stains.

def: A routine professional cleaning that prevents oral disease.
ex: A twice-yearly {{dental prophylaxis}} keeps gums healthy.

def: A hole in a tooth caused by progressive decay.
ex: The X-ray revealed a {{dental cavity}} between two molars.

def: A sticky bacterial film that forms on teeth and causes decay.
ex: Daily brushing removes {{plaque}} before it hardens.

def: Hardened mineralised plaque firmly attached to tooth surfaces.
ex: The hygienist scaled away heavy {{calculus}} deposits.

def: A pointed tooth between the incisors and premolars used for tearing.
ex: The pointed {{canine tooth}} sat high in the arch.

def: The soft inner tissue containing nerves and blood vessels of a tooth.
ex: Deep decay inflamed the {{dental pulp}}, causing throbbing pain.

def: A durable silver-coloured restoration made from a metal alloy.
ex: The old {{amalgam filling}} was replaced with tooth-coloured composite.

def: A tooth-coloured resin restoration bonded directly into a cavity.
ex: She preferred a {{composite filling}} for its natural appearance.

def: A custom restoration cemented within the cusps of a prepared tooth.
ex: A ceramic {{dental inlay}} restored the chewing surface precisely.

def: A restoration covering one or more cusps of a damaged tooth.
ex: The fractured cusp was rebuilt with a gold {{dental onlay}}.

def: Partial removal of the pulp, commonly performed on primary teeth.
ex: The paediatric dentist performed a {{pulpotomy}} on the baby molar.

def: Surgical removal of a tooth's root tip and surrounding infected tissue.
ex: When retreatment failed, the endodontist did an {{apicoectomy}}.

def: Surgical excision of diseased or overgrown gum tissue.
ex: A {{gingivectomy}} removed the excess tissue covering the crown.

def: Smoothing root surfaces to remove bacterial toxins in gum treatment.
ex: Deep {{root planing}} helped the inflamed pockets to heal.

def: A routine cleaning to remove plaque, calculus and surface stains.
ex: Her six-month {{dental prophy}} kept her gums healthy.

def: Recording how upper and lower teeth meet for prosthetic work.
ex: The technician used the {{bite registration}} to mount the models.

def: A negative mould of the teeth used to make dental appliances.
ex: The dentist took a {{dental impression}} for the new crown.

def: A small attachment bonded to a tooth to hold an orthodontic wire.
ex: One {{dental bracket}} came loose and had to be rebonded.

def: The metal wire connecting brackets and guiding tooth movement.
ex: The orthodontist replaced the {{archwire}} with a stiffer one.

def: An appliance holding space open after early loss of a baby tooth.
ex: A {{space maintainer}} preserved room for the erupting permanent tooth.

def: A custom appliance worn to protect teeth from grinding at night.
ex: The dentist made a {{night guard}} to stop his bruxism damage.

def: A pin set into a root canal to retain a core and crown.
ex: A fibre {{dental post}} reinforced the heavily restored tooth.

def: A connector joining an implant or tooth to a prosthesis.
ex: The crown was screwed onto the implant {{abutment}}.

def: Placement of bone material to rebuild jaw volume before implants.
ex: A {{bone graft}} augmented the ridge before implant placement.

def: A surgical procedure adding bone below the maxillary sinus for implants.
ex: A {{sinus lift}} created enough bone for upper implants.

def: Cleaning between teeth with thread to remove plaque and debris.
ex: Daily {{flossing}} reaches plaque a toothbrush cannot.

def: A radiographic image revealing hidden decay, roots and bone levels.
ex: A {{dental X-ray}} showed decay under the old crown.

def: The natural grooves on a molar's chewing surface prone to decay.
ex: Sealant was applied to protect the {{pit and fissure}} surfaces.

def: The dental specialty caring for the oral health of children.
ex: Nervous young patients are treated in {{pediatric dentistry}}.

def: A short-term cap protecting a prepared tooth until the permanent crown.
ex: A {{temporary crown}} shielded the tooth for two weeks.

def: Improper positioning of teeth relative to one another in the dental arch.
ex: Severe {{malalignment}} of the anterior teeth required fixed orthodontic treatment.

def: Presence of gaps between adjacent teeth within the dental arch.
ex: Generalised {{spacing}} between the upper incisors was closed with braces.

def: Excessive vertical overlap of the lower incisors by the upper incisors.
ex: The patient's {{deep bite}} caused the lower incisors to contact the palate.

def: Orthodontic device bonded to teeth that the patient cannot remove.
ex: A {{fixed appliance}} was bonded to correct the rotated premolars.

def: Orthodontic device the patient can insert and remove for cleaning.
ex: A {{removable appliance}} was used to expand the narrow upper arch.

def: Appliance that harnesses muscle forces to modify jaw growth and position.
ex: A {{functional appliance}} advanced the mandible in the growing patient.

def: Extraoral device using the head or neck as anchorage for tooth movement.
ex: {{Headgear}} was prescribed at night to restrain maxillary growth.

def: Appliance that widens the upper jaw by separating the palatal suture.
ex: A {{palatal expander}} corrected the posterior crossbite in six weeks.

def: Metal ring cemented around a tooth to anchor an appliance.
ex: An {{orthodontic band}} was fitted to the first molar for the headgear.

def: Attaching brackets directly to enamel using adhesive resin.
ex: Direct {{bonding}} of the brackets was completed in a single visit.

def: Small wire or elastic tie securing the archwire into a bracket.
ex: An elastomeric {{ligature}} held the wire firmly in the slot.

def: Rubber band applying force between arches to guide tooth movement.
ex: The patient wore an {{orthodontic elastic}} to correct the Class II relationship.

def: Removing thin enamel between teeth to create space for alignment.
ex: {{Interproximal reduction}} provided space to relieve the mild crowding.

def: Measurement of skull landmarks on radiographs to plan orthodontic treatment.
ex: {{Cephalometric analysis}} revealed a skeletal Class II jaw relationship.

def: Plaster replica of the teeth used for diagnosis and planning.
ex: The {{study model}} was measured to assess arch length discrepancy.

def: Selective grinding of tooth surfaces to improve how teeth meet.
ex: An {{occlusal adjustment}} removed the premature contact on the molar.

def: Controlled repositioning of teeth through applied orthodontic forces.
ex: Light forces produce more physiological {{tooth movement}} than heavy ones.

def: Resistance to unwanted tooth movement used to control applied forces.
ex: Maximum {{anchorage}} was needed to retract the upper incisors.

def: Return of teeth toward their original position after treatment ends.
ex: Lower incisor {{relapse}} occurred because the retainer was not worn.

def: Measures such as retainers that keep teeth in the corrected position.
ex: Long-term retainer wear is essential for effective {{relapse prevention}}.

def: Channel in a bracket into which the archwire is engaged.
ex: The archwire was seated fully into the 0.022-inch {{bracket slot}}.

def: Bracket with a built-in clip that holds the wire without ties.
ex: A {{self-ligating bracket}} reduced friction during initial alignment.

def: Fixed appliances bonded to the tongue side of the teeth.
ex: {{Lingual braces}} were chosen because they are hidden from view.

def: Tooth-coloured bracket made of ceramic for improved aesthetics.
ex: A {{ceramic bracket}} blends with the enamel and is less visible.

def: Connected elastic modules used to close spaces between teeth.
ex: A {{power chain}} was stretched across the arch to close the gaps.

def: Spring placed on the archwire to open or close space.
ex: An open {{coil spring}} created room for the blocked-out canine.

def: Small elastic or wire placed between teeth to create space for bands.
ex: A {{separator}} was inserted a week before the molar bands were fitted.

def: Metal ring cemented around a molar to secure appliance attachments.
ex: The {{molar band}} carried the tube that received the headgear.

def: Small tube on a molar band or attachment that holds the archwire end.
ex: The archwire was inserted into the {{buccal tube}} on the last molar.

def: Wire crossing the palate to connect molars and reinforce anchorage.
ex: A {{transpalatal arch}} stabilised the molars during incisor retraction.

def: Wire along the tongue side of lower teeth to maintain arch space.
ex: A lower {{lingual arch}} preserved the leeway space during the transition.

def: Appliance holding the lip away from teeth to gain arch length.
ex: A {{lip bumper}} uprighted the lower molars and relieved crowding.

def: Fixed appliance with four helices used to widen the upper arch.
ex: A {{quad helix}} slowly expanded the constricted maxillary arch.

def: Quick widening of the upper jaw by splitting the midpalatal suture.
ex: {{Rapid palatal expansion}} opened a diastema as the suture separated.

def: Removable acrylic plate that opens the bite to reduce deep overbite.
ex: An anterior {{bite plate}} disengaged the posterior teeth for correction.

def: Removable retainer with an acrylic base and a labial bow wire.
ex: A {{Hawley retainer}} held the corrected teeth after debonding.

def: Fine wire bonded behind the teeth to hold them permanently.
ex: A lower {{bonded retainer}} prevented relapse of the aligned incisors.

def: Clear vacuum-formed plastic retainer covering the whole arch.
ex: An {{Essix retainer}} was worn at night to maintain the result.

def: Two-part functional appliance that posture the mandible forward.
ex: A {{Twin Block appliance}} advanced the lower jaw in the young patient.

def: Removable functional appliance that repositions the jaw for growth guidance.
ex: An {{activator appliance}} was used to treat the Class II malocclusion.

def: Functional appliance that shields the cheeks and lips to shape the arches.
ex: A {{Frankel appliance}} removed abnormal muscle pressure from the arches.

def: Compact functional appliance guiding mandibular position and growth.
ex: A {{bionator}} was prescribed to encourage forward mandibular growth.

def: Fixed functional appliance holding the mandible continuously forward.
ex: A {{Herbst appliance}} corrected the overjet without patient compliance.

def: Extraoral appliance that pulls the upper jaw forward using facial support.
ex: A {{face mask}} advanced the maxilla in the Class III patient.

def: Headgear pulling the maxilla forward to correct underdeveloped upper jaw.
ex: {{Reverse-pull headgear}} was used to protract the retruded maxilla.

def: Headgear anchored at the neck to restrain maxillary forward growth.
ex: {{Cervical headgear}} held back the maxilla during active growth.

def: Metal bow linking molar tubes to an extraoral headgear force.
ex: The {{facebow}} inserted into the molar tubes and connected to the strap.

def: Buccolingual root inclination controlled by the archwire in the slot.
ex: Additional {{torque}} was placed to move the incisor roots palatally.

def: Loss of root length that may follow orthodontic force application.
ex: Apical {{root resorption}} was noted on the upper incisors after treatment.

def: Removal of bonded brackets and adhesive at the end of treatment.
ex: After {{debonding}}, the residual adhesive was polished off the enamel.

def: Flexible superelastic archwire used in the initial aligning stage.
ex: A {{nickel-titanium wire}} gently levelled the crowded lower arch.

def: Rigid archwire used for space closure and finishing stages.
ex: A rectangular {{stainless steel wire}} was placed for space closure.

def: Initial phase correcting tooth rotations and vertical discrepancies.
ex: {{Leveling and aligning}} straightened the arch before space closure began.

def: Bringing teeth together to eliminate extraction or residual spaces.
ex: {{Space closure}} was carried out after the premolars were extracted.

def: Mismatch between the dental midline and the facial midline.
ex: A 2 mm {{midline discrepancy}} was corrected using asymmetric elastics.

def: System categorising malocclusion by the relationship of the first permanent molars.
ex: By {{Angle classification}}, the case was recorded as Class II division 1.

def: Lower molar positioned distally, with the upper jaw relatively forward.
ex: The {{Class II malocclusion}} presented with an increased overjet.

def: Lower molar positioned mesially, often with a protruded lower jaw.
ex: The {{Class III malocclusion}} showed a reverse incisor relationship.

def: Mismatch in the size or position of the upper and lower jaws.
ex: A large {{skeletal discrepancy}} required combined orthodontic and surgical care.

def: Tooth inclination that masks an underlying skeletal jaw discrepancy.
ex: Natural {{dental compensation}} camouflaged the mild skeletal imbalance.

def: Planned sequential removal of teeth to relieve severe crowding early.
ex: {{Serial extraction}} was started to manage the marked incisor crowding.

def: Early treatment that intercepts a developing malocclusion in children.
ex: {{Interceptive orthodontics}} corrected the crossbite in the mixed dentition.

def: Side-view skull radiograph used for orthodontic cephalometric analysis.
ex: A {{lateral cephalogram}} was traced to measure the jaw angles.

def: Outline of skull landmarks drawn from a cephalometric radiograph.
ex: The {{cephalometric tracing}} located the key skeletal reference points.

def: Comparison of upper and lower tooth widths to assess ratio harmony.
ex: {{Bolton analysis}} revealed a tooth-size discrepancy in the lower arch.

def: Difference between space available and space needed in the arch.
ex: A negative {{arch length discrepancy}} indicated crowding of the teeth.

def: Extra space from primary molars being wider than their successors.
ex: The {{leeway space}} was preserved to allow the permanent teeth to align.

def: Prediction of space needed for unerupted teeth in children.
ex: A {{mixed dentition analysis}} estimated space for the erupting canines.

def: Small screw placed in bone to provide fixed orthodontic anchorage.
ex: A {{temporary anchorage device}} allowed intrusion of the overerupted molar.

def: Tiny bone screw used as a stable point of skeletal anchorage.
ex: An {{orthodontic mini-screw}} was inserted between the roots for retraction.

def: Backward movement of teeth toward the rear of the arch.
ex: Molar {{distalization}} created space to correct the Class II relationship.

def: Movement of a tooth deeper into its bony socket.
ex: {{Intrusion}} of the upper incisors reduced the deep overbite.

def: Movement of a tooth outward from its socket toward the occlusal plane.
ex: Controlled {{extrusion}} brought the impacted canine into the arch.

def: Turning a tooth around its long axis to correct its position.
ex: Correcting the {{tooth rotation}} of the premolar improved the arch form.

def: Tipping a mesially inclined molar back to an upright position.
ex: {{Molar uprighting}} restored space lost after the early extraction.

def: Forward tipping of the incisor crowns relative to the jaw.
ex: {{Proclination}} of the lower incisors relieved the mild crowding.

def: Stage after active treatment when retainers stabilise the corrected teeth.
ex: During the {{retention phase}}, the patient wore retainers every night.

def: Elastic bands stretched between the upper and lower arches.
ex: {{Intermaxillary elastics}} corrected the anteroposterior jaw relationship.

def: Measuring gum pocket depth around teeth with a graduated probe.
ex: The hygienist recorded pocket depths during {{periodontal probing}}.

def: Distance from the gum margin to the base of the pocket.
ex: A {{probing depth}} over four millimetres suggests disease.

def: Loss of the connective tissue attachment supporting a tooth.
ex: Radiographs confirmed severe {{clinical attachment loss}}.

def: Bleeding from the gum triggered by gentle probe insertion.
ex: {{Bleeding on probing}} indicates active gingival inflammation.

def: Removing deposits from tooth surfaces below the gum line.
ex: The therapist performed {{subgingival scaling}} under local anaesthetic.

def: Lifting the gum to access and clean root surfaces surgically.
ex: Advanced pockets were treated with {{flap surgery}}.

def: Using a barrier membrane to rebuild lost periodontal tissues.
ex: The defect was managed with {{guided tissue regeneration}}.

def: Destruction of the bone that surrounds and supports teeth.
ex: The X-ray revealed extensive {{alveolar bone loss}}.

def: Bone loss reaching where multi-rooted tooth roots divide.
ex: Grade two {{furcation involvement}} complicated the treatment.

def: Transplanted tissue used to augment thin or receded gums.
ex: A {{gingival graft}} covered the exposed root surface.

def: Grafting palatal connective tissue to thicken or cover gums.
ex: The recession was treated with a {{connective tissue graft}}.

def: Detached palatal tissue placed to widen keratinized gingiva.
ex: A {{free gingival graft}} increased the attached tissue band.

def: Tough gum tissue that resists mechanical and chewing forces.
ex: Adequate {{keratinized gingiva}} protects the tooth margin.

def: Firm gum bound tightly to the underlying alveolar bone.
ex: A narrow zone of {{attached gingiva}} needed grafting.

def: Line where attached gum meets the loose oral mucosa.
ex: The incision followed the {{mucogingival junction}}.

def: The edge of the gum tissue surrounding the tooth crown.
ex: Plaque accumulated along the {{gingival margin}}.

def: Regular recall cleanings that control treated gum disease.
ex: She attends {{periodontal maintenance}} every three months.

def: Surgically restoring gum tissue over an exposed tooth root.
ex: The graft achieved complete {{root coverage}}.

def: Removing gum or bone to expose more tooth structure.
ex: {{Crown lengthening}} exposed enough tooth for a restoration.

def: Reshaping bone defects to create healthier gum contours.
ex: {{Osseous surgery}} eliminated the deep bony pocket.

def: Abnormal overgrowth of gum tissue from drugs or plaque.
ex: Certain medications cause noticeable {{gingival enlargement}}.

def: Localized pus collection within an inflamed periodontal pocket.
ex: The swelling proved to be a {{periodontal abscess}}.

def: Formation and discharge of pus from inflamed gum tissue.
ex: {{Suppuration}} appeared when the pocket was pressed.

def: A bone pocket whose base lies below adjacent bone crest.
ex: The {{infrabony defect}} was suitable for regeneration.

def: A thin graduated instrument for measuring gum pocket depth.
ex: He inserted the {{periodontal probe}} gently into the sulcus.

def: An area-specific hand instrument for subgingival root cleaning.
ex: She debrided the pocket with a {{Gracey curette}}.

def: A powered device removing deposits via vibrating tips and water.
ex: The {{ultrasonic scaler}} flushed away calculus and debris.

def: An organized bacterial community adhering to tooth surfaces.
ex: Mature {{biofilm}} drives periodontal tissue destruction.

def: Tissue attaching the gum to the tooth surface near the crown.
ex: Inflammation shifts the {{junctional epithelium}} apically.

def: A protein gel promoting regeneration of periodontal attachment.
ex: The surgeon applied {{enamel matrix derivative}} to the defect.

def: A film that excludes epithelium so bone and ligament regenerate.
ex: A resorbable {{barrier membrane}} covered the grafted site.

def: Injury to supporting tissues from excessive biting forces.
ex: {{Occlusal trauma}} widened the periodontal ligament space.

def: Inflammation with bone loss around a dental implant.
ex: Untreated {{peri-implantitis}} threatened the implant survival.

def: Reversible gum inflammation around an implant without bone loss.
ex: {{Peri-implant mucositis}} resolved after professional cleaning.

def: Mechanical removal of plaque and deposits from root surfaces.
ex: Thorough {{root debridement}} reduced the pocket depth.

def: Therapy that decreases the depth of periodontal pockets.
ex: Surgery achieved significant {{pocket reduction}}.

def: Joining loose teeth together to stabilize them functionally.
ex: Mobile incisors were stabilized by {{periodontal splinting}}.

def: Assessment checking whether the tooth pulp is still alive.
ex: A {{pulp vitality test}} confirmed the nerve had died.

def: Death of the pulp tissue inside the tooth.
ex: Deep decay led to complete {{pulp necrosis}}.

def: Inflammation of tissues surrounding the tooth root tip.
ex: The abscess arose from chronic {{apical periodontitis}}.

def: Filling the cleaned canal space with a sealing material.
ex: Three-dimensional {{root canal obturation}} prevents reinfection.

def: A rubbery material used to fill root canals after cleaning.
ex: The canal was sealed with warm {{gutta-percha}}.

def: A tight closure at the root tip preventing bacterial leakage.
ex: A sound {{apical seal}} ensures long-term success.

def: Flushing the root canal to remove debris and bacteria.
ex: Copious {{canal irrigation}} disinfected the root system.

def: A fine metal instrument used to shape and clean canals.
ex: She advanced the {{endodontic file}} to working length.

def: A twisting instrument that enlarges and widens root canals.
ex: The {{reamer}} was rotated to widen the narrow canal.

def: The central hollow space of the crown housing pulp tissue.
ex: Access was cut into the {{pulp chamber}}.

def: Establishing a smooth path along the full canal length.
ex: Careful {{canal negotiation}} located the calcified passage.

def: The measured canal distance guiding instrumentation and filling.
ex: The {{working length}} was confirmed with an apex locator.

def: The opening at the root tip where nerves and vessels enter.
ex: Filling should stop just short of the {{apical foramen}}.

def: A small branch leaving the main canal toward the root surface.
ex: An untreated {{accessory canal}} harboured residual bacteria.

def: A side channel branching horizontally from the main canal.
ex: Sealer was pushed into a visible {{lateral canal}}.

def: The complex network of canals within a tooth root.
ex: The molar had a branching {{root canal system}}.

def: Using files to clean and shape the root canal walls.
ex: {{Canal instrumentation}} created a smooth tapered form.

def: Combined cleaning and shaping of canals before filling.
ex: {{Biomechanical preparation}} removed infected dentine.

def: A sheet isolating the tooth to keep the field dry and clean.
ex: A {{rubber dam}} isolated the tooth during treatment.

def: A disinfecting irrigant that dissolves tissue inside canals.
ex: {{Sodium hypochlorite}} was used to disinfect the canal.

def: A cement filling gaps between the filling and canal wall.
ex: The {{root canal sealer}} bonded the gutta-percha in place.

def: The main gutta-percha point fitted to the prepared canal.
ex: The {{master cone}} reached the correct working length.

def: The narrowest canal point near the root tip end.
ex: Filling ideally terminates at the {{apical constriction}}.

def: A dark X-ray area at the root tip signalling bone loss.
ex: A {{periapical radiolucency}} confirmed the chronic lesion.

def: Placing medicament directly on exposed pulp to preserve it.
ex: {{Direct pulp capping}} protected the small exposure.

def: Leaving deep dentine and medicating over the near pulp.
ex: {{Indirect pulp capping}} avoided exposing the pulp.

def: An opening reaching the pulp during decay or drilling.
ex: Excavation caused an accidental {{pulp exposure}}.

def: Applying mild current to check whether pulp nerves respond.
ex: The {{electric pulp test}} produced no response.

def: Using cold or heat to assess the tooth pulp response.
ex: A {{thermal pulp test}} triggered lingering pain.

def: Multiple tiny canal branches spreading near the root apex.
ex: An {{apical delta}} made complete cleaning difficult.

def: An antibacterial paste used as a temporary canal dressing.
ex: {{Calcium hydroxide}} was placed between appointments.

def: An opening cut to reach the canals for treatment.
ex: The {{endodontic access cavity}} exposed all canal orifices.

def: A tight closure of the crown preventing bacterial re-entry.
ex: A good {{coronal seal}} protects the completed treatment.

def: An instrument shaping canals with back-and-forth rotation.
ex: A single {{reciprocating file}} prepared the whole canal.

def: Shaping canals with continuously rotating engine-driven files.
ex: {{Rotary instrumentation}} sped up canal preparation.

def: Sealing the root tip from behind during apical surgery.
ex: A {{retrograde filling}} sealed the resected root end.

def: Loss of dentine from inside the canal by inflamed pulp cells.
ex: {{Internal resorption}} hollowed out the canal wall.

def: Loss of tooth structure attacked from the outer root surface.
ex: Trauma triggered progressive {{external resorption}}.

def: A removable prosthesis replacing all natural teeth in one jaw.
ex: The patient received a {{complete denture}} for the upper jaw.

def: A removable prosthesis replacing some but not all missing teeth.
ex: A {{partial denture}} filled the gap left by two missing molars.

def: A non-removable prosthesis anchored to adjacent teeth to replace missing ones.
ex: The dentist cemented a {{fixed bridge}} across the gap.

def: A bridge supported by an abutment on only one side.
ex: A {{cantilever bridge}} was chosen because only one adjacent tooth remained.

def: A tooth that supports and retains a bridge or partial denture.
ex: The molar served as an {{abutment tooth}} for the new bridge.

def: The artificial tooth in a bridge that replaces a missing tooth.
ex: The {{pontic}} matched the shade of the neighbouring teeth.

def: A material used to bond restorations or fill cavities in teeth.
ex: The crown was secured with {{dental cement}}.

def: A tooth-coloured resin material used for fillings and restorations.
ex: The dentist restored the tooth with {{composite resin}}.

def: A cement that bonds chemically to tooth and releases fluoride.
ex: A {{glass ionomer}} was placed to release fluoride near the gum.

def: A ceramic material used for crowns, veneers and prosthetic teeth.
ex: The crown was made of {{porcelain}} for a natural look.

def: A strong white ceramic used for durable crowns and bridges.
ex: The molar crown was milled from {{zirconia}}.

def: A metal mixture used for casting crowns, bridges or frameworks.
ex: The framework was cast from a gold {{dental alloy}}.

def: A substance used to record the shape of teeth and gums.
ex: The tray was loaded with {{impression material}} for the mould.

def: An elastic powder-based material used to take dental impressions.
ex: The assistant mixed {{alginate}} to record the arch.

def: A wax model showing the planned shape of a restoration.
ex: The technician made a {{wax-up}} before casting the crown.

def: A device that reproduces jaw movements to mount dental casts.
ex: The casts were mounted on an {{articulator}} to check the bite.

def: A record of how the upper and lower teeth meet together.
ex: An {{occlusal record}} was taken to mount the models correctly.

def: A set of samples used to match a restoration to tooth colour.
ex: The dentist used a {{shade guide}} to select the crown colour.

def: The shaping of a tooth to receive a fitted crown.
ex: The {{crown preparation}} reduced the tooth to make room for the cap.

def: A facility where crowns, dentures and bridges are fabricated.
ex: The impression was sent to the {{dental laboratory}}.

def: The process of forming a metal restoration by pouring molten alloy.
ex: The {{casting}} produced a precise metal framework.

def: A device emitting light to harden resin-based dental materials.
ex: The filling was set using a {{curing light}}.

def: A resin that helps a restoration adhere to tooth structure.
ex: A {{bonding agent}} was applied before the composite.

def: The part of a denture that rests on the gum tissue.
ex: The {{denture base}} was tinted to resemble the gums.

def: A metal arm that grips a tooth to retain a partial denture.
ex: The {{denture clasp}} held the appliance firmly in place.

def: A plastic material widely used to make denture bases.
ex: The denture base was moulded from pink {{acrylic resin}}.

def: A foundation placed in a root canal to support a crown.
ex: A {{post and core}} rebuilt the tooth before crowning.

def: A durable crown made from a gold-based dental alloy.
ex: The back molar was restored with a {{gold crown}}.

def: The cast metal structure supporting a partial denture.
ex: The {{metal framework}} gave the denture strength and stability.

def: A stage where a restoration is tested for fit before completion.
ex: At the {{try-in}} the denture teeth were checked for position.

def: An accurate model of the mouth used to build a prosthesis.
ex: The crown was fabricated on the {{master cast}}.

def: A hard gypsum material poured into impressions to make models.
ex: The impression was filled with {{dental stone}} to form a cast.

def: A mineral used in dental plasters and stones for models.
ex: Dental models are cast from {{gypsum}} products.

def: A heat-resistant material forming the mould for metal casting.
ex: The wax pattern was surrounded by {{investment material}}.

def: A crown with a metal core covered by porcelain.
ex: A {{metal-ceramic crown}} combined strength with a natural appearance.

def: A crown made entirely of ceramic without any metal.
ex: The front tooth received an {{all-ceramic crown}} for aesthetics.

def: The face height measured when the teeth are in contact.
ex: The dentures restored the patient's {{vertical dimension}}.

def: A reference jaw position with condyles seated in the joints.
ex: The bite was recorded in {{centric relation}}.

def: Adding new material to a denture base to improve fit.
ex: A {{denture reline}} was needed after the gums shrank.

def: A paste or powder used to hold a denture more securely.
ex: She applied {{denture adhesive}} to stop the plate moving.

def: A denture inserted right after teeth are removed.
ex: An {{immediate denture}} was fitted on the day of extraction.

def: A denture that fits over retained roots or implants.
ex: The {{overdenture}} clipped onto two implants for stability.

def: A connector attaching a crown to a dental implant fixture.
ex: The crown was screwed onto the {{implant abutment}}.

def: A cover placed on an implant while the gum heals.
ex: A {{healing cap}} was fitted to shape the soft tissue.

def: The direct bonding of bone to an implant surface.
ex: Successful {{osseointegration}} allowed the implant to bear load.

def: A short-term restoration used until the final one is ready.
ex: A {{provisional restoration}} protected the prepared tooth.

def: The edge where a crown meets the prepared tooth surface.
ex: A well-fitting {{margin}} prevents leakage under the crown.

def: The full placement of a restoration onto its preparation.
ex: Complete {{seating}} of the crown was checked with an X-ray.

def: An acid gel that roughens enamel to improve bonding.
ex: The {{etchant}} was rinsed off before applying the adhesive.

def: An adhesive cement used to bond ceramic and resin restorations.
ex: The veneer was fixed with tooth-coloured {{resin cement}}.

def: A low-viscosity resin used in small or hard-to-reach cavities.
ex: A {{flowable composite}} was placed in the base of the cavity.

def: A thin strip used to shape a filling between teeth.
ex: A {{matrix band}} formed the wall of the composite filling.

def: A protective layer placed under a filling near the pulp.
ex: A {{cavity liner}} shielded the pulp before the restoration.

def: A cement layer placed under a filling to protect the pulp.
ex: A {{base}} was placed to insulate the deep cavity.

def: Applying a thin ceramic or resin facing to a restoration.
ex: The {{veneering}} porcelain gave the crown its natural colour.

def: A fitting stage checking the metal framework before finishing.
ex: At the {{framework try-in}} the fit was verified on the abutments.

def: A wax rim used to record jaw relations for dentures.
ex: The {{bite block}} helped establish the occlusal plane.

def: A prefabricated artificial tooth set into a denture.
ex: Each {{denture tooth}} was selected to match the patient's shade.

def: A metal case used to process a denture in acrylic.
ex: The wax denture was packed into a {{flask}} for curing.

def: An abrasive paste used to smooth and shine restorations.
ex: The technician finished the crown with {{polishing paste}}.

def: A thin glassy layer fired onto porcelain for a smooth finish.
ex: A final {{glaze}} gave the crown a lifelike shine.

def: Heating ceramic particles to fuse them into a solid restoration.
ex: The zirconia crown gained strength during {{sintering}}.

def: Cutting a restoration from a solid block by machine.
ex: The crown was produced by {{milling}} a ceramic block.

def: A digital device that captures 3D images of the teeth.
ex: An {{intraoral scanner}} replaced the traditional impression.

def: A computer-based record of the teeth made by scanning.
ex: A {{digital impression}} was sent electronically to the lab.

def: A band of tooth structure a crown grips for retention.
ex: An adequate {{ferrule}} improves the strength of the crown.

def: A removable device covering teeth to manage bite problems.
ex: An {{occlusal splint}} protected the teeth from grinding.

def: A bridge bonded to teeth by thin metal or ceramic wings.
ex: A {{maryland bridge}} replaced the missing incisor conservatively.

def: A specialist who fabricates dentures, crowns and bridges.
ex: The {{dental technician}} built the crown in the laboratory.

def: An artificial device that replaces missing teeth or structures.
ex: The {{prosthesis}} restored both function and appearance.

def: The resistance of a prosthesis to being displaced from teeth.
ex: Good {{retention}} kept the denture stable while chewing.

def: A recessed area of a tooth that helps retain a clasp.

def: A removable model of a single prepared tooth for lab work.
ex: The crown was waxed directly on the {{die}}.

def: A ceramic coating that masks the metal beneath porcelain.
ex: The {{opaque layer}} hid the grey metal of the coping.

def: The inner shell of a crown that fits over the tooth.

def: Removal of a tooth requiring incision of gum or bone.
ex: The impacted molar needed a {{surgical extraction}} under anaesthesia.

def: Surgical or simple extraction of a third molar tooth.
ex: He booked a {{wisdom tooth removal}} for the aching lower jaw.

def: Painful inflammation of an extraction socket after clot loss.
ex: Postoperative {{alveolar osteitis}} caused severe throbbing pain.

def: Empty extraction socket lacking a protective blood clot.
ex: Smoking increased her risk of a {{dry socket}}.

def: Surgical procedure to increase bone volume for implants.
ex: The thin ridge required {{bone augmentation}} before implant placement.

def: Removal of tissue for laboratory examination and diagnosis.
ex: A {{biopsy}} confirmed the lesion was benign.

def: Complete surgical removal of a cyst with its lining.
ex: The jaw {{cyst enucleation}} preserved the adjacent teeth.

def: Stitching wound edges together to promote healing.
ex: Careful {{suturing}} closed the surgical flap neatly.

def: The process of stopping bleeding during or after surgery.
ex: Pressure achieved {{haemostasis}} at the extraction site.

def: Single film showing the whole upper and lower jaws.
ex: The {{panoramic radiograph}} revealed an unerupted wisdom tooth.

def: X-ray showing crowns of upper and lower teeth together.
ex: A {{bitewing radiograph}} detected caries between the molars.

def: X-ray showing the whole tooth and surrounding bone apex.
ex: The {{periapical film}} showed a shadow around the root tip.

def: Three-dimensional imaging used for detailed dental and jaw planning.
ex: {{Cone beam computed tomography}} mapped the nerve before surgery.

def: Analysing X-ray images to identify disease or abnormalities.
ex: Accurate {{radiographic interpretation}} guided the treatment plan.

def: Protective resin coating applied to grooves of teeth.
ex: A {{fissure sealant}} shielded the child's molar from decay.

def: Concentrated coating painted on teeth to strengthen enamel.
ex: The hygienist applied {{fluoride varnish}} to prevent cavities.

def: The first set of temporary teeth in children.
ex: Caries in the {{primary dentition}} needs early treatment.

def: Stage when both baby and adult teeth are present.
ex: Orthodontic assessment is common during the {{mixed dentition}}.

def: Prefabricated metal cap used to restore children's molars.
ex: A {{stainless steel crown}} protected the decayed baby molar.

def: Injury to teeth or supporting tissues from impact.
ex: A fall caused {{dental trauma}} to the front teeth.

def: The emergence of a tooth through the gum.
ex: Delayed {{tooth eruption}} prompted a radiographic check.

def: Grafting an empty socket to maintain bone after extraction.
ex: {{Socket preservation}} kept the ridge suitable for an implant.

def: A cut made along the ridge crest during surgery.
ex: The surgeon began with a {{crestal incision}} over the ridge.

def: Using membranes to encourage new bone growth surgically.
ex: {{Guided bone regeneration}} rebuilt the deficient ridge.

def: Surgical removal of a frenulum attachment in the mouth.
ex: A {{frenectomy}} corrected the tight lip attachment.

def: Removal of the gum flap over a partly erupted tooth.
ex: An {{operculectomy}} relieved the inflamed tissue over the molar.

def: Removing a tooth crown while leaving roots to protect nerves.
ex: A {{coronectomy}} avoided damaging the inferior alveolar nerve.

def: Instrument used to grasp and remove teeth during extraction.

def: Lever instrument used to loosen a tooth before removal.

def: Surgical cutting of bone to reshape or reposition it.

def: A dark area on an X-ray showing less dense tissue.

def: A light area on an X-ray showing dense material.
ex: The metal filling appeared as a bright {{radiopacity}}.

def: X-ray taken with the film on the biting surface.
ex: An {{occlusal radiograph}} located the extra buried tooth.

def: Side-view skull X-ray used to plan orthodontic treatment.
ex: The {{cephalometric radiograph}} measured the jaw relationship.

def: Amount of ionising radiation received during imaging.
ex: Digital sensors lower the patient's {{radiation dose}}.

def: Protective cover shielding the body during X-rays.
ex: The patient wore a {{lead apron}} during the exposure.

def: Difference in brightness between structures on an image.
ex: Good {{radiographic contrast}} made the caries easy to see.

def: Electronic detector that captures dental X-ray images.
ex: The {{digital sensor}} produced an instant on-screen image.

def: Small benign wart-like growth of the oral mucosa.

def: Fluid-filled sac in the jaw arising from tooth tissues.

def: Cyst surrounding the crown of an unerupted tooth.

def: Transplanting gum tissue to cover roots or thicken gums.
ex: A {{soft tissue graft}} covered the exposed root surface.

def: Section of tissue lifted to access bone or a tooth.
ex: The {{surgical flap}} was reflected to expose the root.

def: Taking out stitches once a surgical wound has healed.
ex: {{Suture removal}} was scheduled seven days after surgery.

def: Enlargement of tissues occurring after a surgical procedure.
ex: An ice pack reduced the {{postoperative swelling}}.

def: Damage to a nerve causing numbness or altered sensation.
ex: Lower wisdom removal carries a risk of {{nerve injury}}.

def: Abnormal tingling or numbness following nerve disturbance.
ex: Temporary {{paraesthesia}} of the lip followed the extraction.

def: Condition where a tooth fails to erupt into position.
ex: The {{impaction}} of the third molar caused recurrent pain.

def: Inducing a barrier at the open root end of immature teeth.
ex: {{Apexification}} closed the open apex of the injured incisor.

def: Preserving pulp to allow continued root development in children.
ex: {{Apexogenesis}} allowed the young root to keep forming.

def: Placing material over exposed pulp to protect and heal it.
ex: Direct {{pulp capping}} preserved the vital pulp tissue.

def: Complete removal of pulp from a primary tooth's canals.
ex: A {{pulpectomy}} treated the abscessed baby molar.

def: A tooth completely knocked out of its socket.
ex: The {{avulsed tooth}} was reimplanted within thirty minutes.

def: Replacing a knocked-out tooth back into its socket.
ex: Prompt {{tooth reimplantation}} saved the young patient's incisor.

def: Device stabilising loose or repositioned teeth after trauma.
ex: A {{dental splint}} held the replanted tooth in place.

def: A break limited to the outer enamel layer of a tooth.
ex: The fall caused a minor {{enamel fracture}} of the incisor.

def: A break involving the visible part of a tooth.
ex: A {{crown fracture}} exposed the dentine of the front tooth.

def: A break located within the root of a tooth.
ex: A radiograph revealed a horizontal {{root fracture}}.

def: A tooth present in a baby's mouth at birth.
ex: The newborn had a {{natal tooth}} on the lower ridge.

def: The process of a baby's first teeth coming through.
ex: {{Teething}} made the infant irritable and dribbly.

def: Soft swelling over a tooth about to emerge.
ex: An {{eruption cyst}} appeared over the erupting molar.

def: Extra tooth located between the upper central incisors.
ex: A {{mesiodens}} blocked the eruption of the front tooth.

def: Rapid tooth decay affecting very young children's teeth.
ex: Frequent bottle feeding led to {{early childhood caries}}.

def: Techniques guiding a child's cooperation during dental care.
ex: Gentle {{behaviour management}} calmed the anxious child.

def: A step-by-step method familiarising children with procedures.
ex: The dentist used {{tell-show-do}} to ease the child's fear.

def: Inhaled gas that relaxes anxious patients during treatment.
ex: {{Nitrous oxide sedation}} helped the child relax in the chair.

def: Developmental enamel defect affecting molars and incisors.
ex: {{Molar incisor hypomineralisation}} left chalky patches on the teeth.

def: Temporary filling placed to stabilise decay in children.
ex: An {{interim therapeutic restoration}} halted the spreading decay.

def: Use of medication to relax a patient during treatment.
ex: {{Dental sedation}} allowed the long procedure to proceed calmly.

def: Abnormal opening between the mouth and maxillary sinus.
ex: An {{oroantral communication}} formed after the upper extraction.

def: Surgical smoothing of the bony ridge before dentures.
ex: An {{alveoloplasty}} reshaped the ridge for a comfortable denture.

def: Cutting an abscess to release accumulated pus.
ex: {{Incision and drainage}} relieved the painful facial swelling.

def: Removal of dead or infected tissue from a wound.
ex: {{Surgical debridement}} cleaned the infected extraction site.

def: Scraping diseased tissue from the inner gum wall.
ex: {{Gingival curettage}} removed inflamed tissue from the pocket.
